labs.actionRequired: Hold therapy if K+ >5.0 mmol/L, restart at 10mg daily when K+ ≤5.0 mmol/L
Discontinue if eGFR falls below 25 ml/min per 1.73 m²
labs.clinicalContext: Minimum eGFR ≥25 ml/min per 1.73 m² required for initiation
Indication requires albuminuria ≥30 mg/g (≥3 mg/mmol) despite maximum tolerated RAS inhibitor

labRequirements.specialPopulationNotes: FDA approved initiation threshold: serum potassium <5.0 mmol/l. FIDELIO-DKD required K+ <4.8 mmol/l at screening. Higher incidence of hyperkalemia reported with steroidal MRAs.
labRequirements.sourceSnippet: We suggest a nonsteroidal mineralocorticoid receptor antagonist with proven kidney or cardiovascular benefit for patients with T2D, an eGFR >=25 ml/min per 1.73 m2, normal serum potassium concentration, and albuminuria (>=30 mg/g [>=3 mg/mmol]) despite maximum tolerated dose of RAS inhibitor (2A). Monitor K+ at 1 month after initiation and then every 4 months.

governance.sourceDocument: KDIGO 2022 Clinical Practice Guideline for Diabetes Management in Chronic Kidney Disease

labs.frequency: at 1 month after initiation and then every 4 months
labs.actionRequired: Hold if K+ >5.0 mmol/L, restart 10 mg daily when K+ ≤5.0 mmol/L
Adjust dose based on eGFR level
labs.clinicalContext: Dosing based on eGFR: 10 mg daily if eGFR 25-59 ml/min per 1.73 m2, 20 mg daily if eGFR ≥60 ml/min per 1.73 m2

labRequirements.specialPopulationNotes: Currently the only nonsteroidal MRA with proven clinical kidney and cardiovascular benefits. FIDELIO-DKD and FIGARO-DKD trial data available.
labRequirements.sourceSnippet: finerenone is currently the only nonsteroidal mineralocorticoid receptor antagonist (MRA) with proven clinical kidney and cardiovascular benefits. K+ ≤4.8 mmol/L - Initiate finerenone 10 mg daily if eGFR 25–59 ml/min per 1.73 m2, 20 mg daily if eGFR ≥60 ml/min per 1.73 m2. Monitor K+ at 1 month after initiation and then every 4 months.

governance.sourceDocument: KDIGO 2022 Clinical Practice Guideline for Diabetes Management in Chronic Kidney Disease
